title: Living beyond the diagnosis: A holistic approach to cancer care
summary: The patient will be advised a well-balanced diet which can help to strengthen and enhance immunity, promote healing, and reduce the chances of nutritional deficiencies. Apart from nutrition, the patient will also be recommended physiotherapy and exercise...
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
On the occasion of Doctor's Day, Dr Amit Chakraborty, Head and Neck Surgeon and Surgical Oncologist at SSO Hospital, Mumbai, highlights the importance of looking beyond conventional cancer treatment to deliver patient-centred care

The patient will be advised a well-balanced diet which can help to strengthen and enhance immunity, promote healing, and reduce the chances of nutritional deficiencies. Apart from nutrition, the patient will also be recommended physiotherapy and exercise under the expert’s guidance to improve energy levels, reduce fatigue, gain muscle strength, and support recovery after surgery or even chemotherapy.

Even mental health is also taken into consideration and is another key aspect of holistic care. Anxiety, depression, and fear can be seen in a large number of cancer patients. The patients can also be advised to use counselling, support groups, mindfulness, yoga, meditation, and relaxation techniques like deep breathing exercises to de-stress and improve their emotional well-being. The patient will also require the family’s support during this crucial phase. Hence, the patient should be assisted with help in doing the daily chores with ease.

The family can accompany the patient during check-ups and follow-ups, and ensure timely vaccination. Moreover, the family should motivate the cancer patient so that they feel calm and composed.

The holistic approach also involves managing treatment-related side effects such as pain, nausea, sleep disturbances, and fatigue for maintaining a good quality of life. Early palliative care, when needed, focuses on relieving symptoms and improving comfort alongside cancer treatment. Regular follow-ups, rehabilitation, and survivorship programmes can help the patients stay healthy.

Conclusion

With a holistic approach that combines advanced medical treatment, healthy nutrition, emotional support, physical rehabilitation, and compassionate care, it is possible for patients to lead a healthy life. It is time to also opt for holistic care and improve the patient’s well-being.
